Package nonprofitbookkeeping.model

Class AccountingTransaction

java.lang.Object
nonprofitbookkeeping.model.AccountingTransaction
All Implemented Interfaces:
Serializable
public class AccountingTransaction extends Object implements Serializable

  • Constructor Details

    • AccountingTransaction

      public AccountingTransaction()
      Constructor AccountingTransaction.

    • AccountingTransaction

      public AccountingTransaction(Account account, Set<AccountingEntry> entries, @Nullable Map<String,String> info, long bookingDateTimestamp)
      Constructs an AccountingTransaction with specified details. Ensures that entries are not null and the transaction is balanced. Note: The process of setting the transaction back onto its entries is commented out to avoid circular dependency issues during construction and may need to be handled post-construction or via a builder pattern.
      Parameters:
      account - The primary account associated with this transaction. Must not be null.
      entries - The set of accounting entries for this transaction. Must not be null and must contain at least two entries.
      info - Optional map of additional information about the transaction. Can be null.
      bookingDateTimestamp - The timestamp when the transaction was booked.
      Throws:
      NullPointerException - if account or entries are null.
      IllegalArgumentException - if entries is empty, contains less than two entries, or if the transaction is not balanced.

  • Method Details

    • getEntries

      public Set<AccountingEntry> getEntries()
      Gets the set of accounting entries that make up this transaction.
      Returns:
      An unmodifiable set of accounting entries.

    • setEntries

      public void setEntries(Set<AccountingEntry> entries)
      Sets the accounting entries for this transaction. Note: If immutability is desired after construction, this method might be restricted or handled by a builder.
      Parameters:
      entries - The set of accounting entries to set.

    • getInfo

      public Map<String,String> getInfo()
      Gets the additional information or metadata associated with this transaction.
      Returns:
      An unmodifiable map of information, or an empty map if no info is set.

    • setInfo

      public void setInfo(Map<String,String> info)
      Sets the additional information for this transaction.
      Parameters:
      info - A map of key-value pairs representing additional information.

    • getBookingDateTimestamp

      public Long getBookingDateTimestamp()
      Gets the booking date timestamp of the transaction. This is typically represented as milliseconds since the epoch.
      Returns:
      The booking date timestamp.

    • setBookingDateTimestamp

      public void setBookingDateTimestamp(long bookingDateTimestamp)
      Sets the booking date timestamp for this transaction.
      Parameters:
      bookingDateTimestamp - The timestamp in milliseconds since epoch.

    • getSerialversionuid

      public static long getSerialversionuid()
      Gets the serial version UID for serialization.
      Returns:
      The serial version UID.

    • getMemo

      public String getMemo()
      Gets the memo or description of the transaction.
      Returns:
      The transaction memo.

    • getTotalAmount

      public BigDecimal getTotalAmount()
      Calculates the total debit amount of the transaction. Iterates through all entries and sums the amounts of debit entries.
      Returns:
      The total debit amount as a BigDecimal. Returns BigDecimal.ZERO if entries are null.

    • isBalanced

      public boolean isBalanced()
      Checks if the transaction is balanced. A transaction is balanced if the sum of all debit entries equals the sum of all credit entries. An empty or null set of entries is considered unbalanced (or could be defined as balanced based on rules).
      Returns:
      true if the transaction is balanced, false otherwise.

    • toString

      public String toString()
      Returns a string representation of the transaction, including its booking date and entries.
      Overrides:
      toString in class Object
      Returns:
      A string summary of the transaction.

    • getDescription

      public String getDescription()
      Gets the description of the transaction, which is equivalent to its memo.
      Returns:
      The transaction description (memo).

    • setDescription

      public void setDescription(String description)
      Sets the description (memo) of the transaction.
      Parameters:
      description - The description to set.

    • getDate

      public String getDate()
      Gets the date of the transaction.
      Returns:
      The transaction date string.

    • setDate

      public void setDate(String date)
      Sets the date of the transaction.
      Parameters:
      date - The date string to set (e.g., "YYYY-MM-DD").

    • getSupplementalLines

      public List<TxnSupplementalLineBase> getSupplementalLines()
      Gets the supplemental lines.
      Returns:
      the supplemental lines

    • setSupplementalLines

      public void setSupplementalLines(List<TxnSupplementalLineBase> supplementalLines)
      Sets the supplemental lines.
      Parameters:
      supplementalLines - the new supplemental lines

    • setMemo

      public void setMemo(String memo)
      Sets the memo for this transaction. This is an alias for setDescription(String).
      Parameters:
      memo - The memo text to set.

    • getId

      public int getId()
      Gets the id.
      Returns:
      the id

    • addEntry

      public void addEntry(AccountingEntry accountingEntry)
      Adds the entry.
      Parameters:
      accountingEntry - the accounting entry

    • setId

      public void setId(int i)
      Sets the id.
      Parameters:
      i - the new id

    • setBookingDateTimestamp

      public void setBookingDateTimestamp(Timestamp from)
      Sets the booking date timestamp.
      Parameters:
      from - the new booking date timestamp

    • getCredit

      public BigDecimal getCredit()
      Returns the total credit amount for this transaction. If explicit credit and debit fields were not populated (e.g. older transactions loaded from disk), the values are computed on demand from the underlying entries collection.
      Returns:
      The total credit amount of the transaction.

    • getDebit

      public BigDecimal getDebit()
      Returns the total debit amount for this transaction. Like getCredit(), this method falls back to computing the value from the transaction's entries if the stored field is empty.
      Returns:
      The total debit amount of the transaction.

    • getToFrom

      public String getToFrom()
      Gets the to from.
      Returns:
      the toFrom

    • setToFrom

      public void setToFrom(String toFrom)
      Sets the to from.
      Parameters:
      toFrom - the toFrom to set

      public String getCheckNumber()
      Gets the check number.
      Returns:
      the checkNumber

    • setCheckNumber

      public void setCheckNumber(String checkNumber)
      Sets the check number.
      Parameters:
      checkNumber - the checkNumber to set

      Count account balance.
